title: Boat Rental in Alicante: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
If you want to hire a boat in Alicante without breaking the bank, expect to pay from €180 for a half-day motorboat or €60 per person on a shared tour. Most departures leave from the Real Club de Regatas or Marina Deportiva, and you can book directly through BoatHire24, which compares verified listings from local owners. With a bit of planning, a day on the water becomes your cheapest yet most memorable excursion.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Alicante?

Prices start at around €60 per person for a shared tour and rise to €600 for a crewed yacht day charter. The exact figure depends on boat size, season, and whether you hire a skipper. Below is a typical price-band breakdown in euros for 2026:

  • Shared tour (RIB or small sailboat, 3, 4 hours): from €60 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 people, no licence): from €180
  • Catamaran full-day (8, 12 guests, crewed): from €400
  • Crewed motor yacht (6, 10 guests, full day): from €600

Prices often include fuel and basic safety gear, but check with the operator. Book early for the best rates and avoid paying the walk-up premium at the marina.

Where to set off in Alicante

Alicante’s coastline offers several well-equipped starting points. The main hub is Puerto de Alicante, a large commercial and leisure harbour with chandlers and cafés. Nearby, the Real Club de Regatas provides a more sheltered base with direct access to the open sea. On the northern edge, Cabo de las Huertas marks the start of the rocky coves that define the Costa Blanca. Further south, Santa Pola marina and Guardamar del Segura give quick routes to the Tabarca Island marine reserve. For a quieter launch, Campello harbour is ideal for exploring the coves towards Villajoyosa.

Which boat to rent

  1. RIB or small speedboat, best for exploring Tabarca Island in under 40 minutes; seats 4, 6; from €180 half-day.
  2. Bowrider or day cruiser, ideal for families wanting a picnic on the water; seats 5, 7; from €250 half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht, perfect for couples who want a peaceful cruise; seats 4, 6; from €200 half-day (skippered).
  4. Catamaran, great for groups seeking stability and space; seats 8, 12; from €400 full-day.
  5. Motor yacht, the luxury option for entertaining; seats 8, 10; from €600 full-day with crew.

For a reliable all-rounder, check the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page which sleeps a small group and is easy to handle if you hold a coastal licence.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Alicante?

For boats under 6 metres and 15 hp you can drive without a licence in Spanish waters; anything larger requires a recognised qualification. The Spanish authorities accept most ICC (International Certificate of Competence) endorsements, and many hire companies also offer a skipper for an extra fee. The operator will confirm the exact requirements when you book, so you can plan accordingly.

If you visit during the high season (July, August) expect the Poniente wind to pick up in the afternoon. Most experienced skippers recommend setting out early and returning before 15:00 to avoid choppy seas.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €600 per person or per boat
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Alicante

Can I rent a boat in Alicante without a licence?

Yes, you can drive a small boat with an engine under 15 hp and a length under 6 metres without a licence.

What is the best month for boat hire in Alicante?

May, June and September offer warm weather and fewer crowds, with calmer winds than the summer peak.

How far is Tabarca Island from Alicante?

Tabarca Island lies about 11 nautical miles south-east of Alicante harbour, a 30, 40 minute boat ride at cruising speed.

Is it cheaper to book a boat online or at the marina?

Booking online through a marketplace like BoatHire24 usually secures a lower rate, and you can compare owner prices in advance.

With affordable half-day rentals and a huge choice of boats, Alicante remains one of the best-value spots in the Mediterranean. Browse day charters in Alicante to compare verified listings and see what fits your budget. If you own a boat, consider listing it on the BoatHire24 marketplace to reach thousands of holidaymakers looking for a smart day out.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Alicante and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
